title: sinocarum pityophilum
intro: Sinocarum pityophilum, a type of flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia, is a member of the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is also known as "Indian carrot." It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sinocarum pityophilum include relief from fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Sinocarum pityophilum is often used as a powder, decoction, or oil, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the skin.
intro_study: According to "Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology", Sinocarum pityophilum, which is a part of the herbal preparation Sinulan forte<sup>®</sup>, has been found to be a safe and effective treatment for acute postviral rhinosinusitis (APRS), resulting in significantly lower absolute post-treatment scores and better relative improvement in total symptom scores and other symptoms.
